task_completion.description: Open an application to serve as the engineering record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad, TextEdit, VSCode), word processor, or notes application is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Determine the Wye phase voltage by dividing the line voltage (415 V) by the square root of 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculator app, Python terminal/script, or search engine showing the calculation '415 / sqrt(3)' or '415 / 1.732'.

task_completion.description: Determine the Delta phase current by dividing the line current (63 A) by the square root of 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculator app, Python terminal/script, or search engine showing the calculation '63 / sqrt(3)' or '63 / 1.732'.

task_completion.description: Record the calculated Wye phase voltage in the engineering record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value 239.6 (or a value between 232.4 and 246.8 to account for the 3% tolerance) is typed into the text editor/document, labeled as Wye phase voltage or similar.

task_completion.description: Record the calculated Delta phase current in the engineering record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value 36.37 (or a value between 35.28 and 37.46 to account for the 3% tolerance) is typed into the text editor/document, labeled as Delta phase current or similar.

integrity.description: The agent actually performs the calculation rather than outputting a pre-programmed answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show a calculator, script, or search query being used to compute the values. The answers should not appear instantly without a visible computation step.

integrity.description: The agent records the values in a persistent document or text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final values are written in a text editor, word processor, or saved file, rather than just being left in a temporary calculator display or terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent uses the exact given values from the task description for its calculations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The calculations explicitly use 415 V and 63 A. The agent does not hallucinate other standard voltages (like 480 V or 400 V) or currents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires calculating 3-phase line-to-phase conversions. The supply is 415 V line-to-line and 63 A line current. The expected Wye (star) phase voltage is 239.6 V (acceptable range with 3% tolerance: 232.4 V to 246.8 V). The expected Delta phase current is 36.37 A (acceptable range with 3% tolerance: 35.28 A to 37.46 A). The formulas are V_phase = V_line / √3 and I_phase = I_line / √3.
